seoDescription: Qualify for Paraguay residency by investing from USD 200,000 in regulated financial instruments held for two years. The passive, hands-off route. Talk to Lincoln.

faqs.question: How much do I need to invest, and for how long?
faqs.answer: The financial instruments route requires at least USD 200,000 (or the guaraní equivalent) deployed into qualifying regulated instruments, maintained for a minimum of two years.

faqs.question: What instruments qualify?
faqs.answer: Sources indicate qualifying instruments are those regulated by the securities superintendency (Superintendencia de Valores) of the Central Bank of Paraguay — broadly corporate bonds, equities, certificates of deposit, and investment funds. The precise approved list is being finalised in the regulation of Resolution 0283/2026, so the exact eligible instruments should be confirmed with counsel before committing.

faqs.question: Do I have to actively manage anything?
faqs.answer: No. There is no business plan, job-creation, or active-management requirement — this is the only purely passive route to direct permanent residency under the Investor Pass. You hold the qualifying instruments for the required period and file annual compliance reporting.

faqs.question: How is the investment evidenced?
faqs.answer: By a certificate from an entity authorised by the Central Bank, confirming the value and the minimum two-year term, dated within 180 days of the application. We prepare and verify this with you.

faqs.question: What are the honest risks?
faqs.answer: The capital is locked for two years and the instruments can fluctuate; Paraguay’s capital market is comparatively small and less liquid than major exchanges, so options are more limited. We size and structure the holding to keep you comfortably above the threshold throughout.

blocks.lead: Deploy qualifying capital into regulated Paraguayan instruments, hold it for the required period, and earn direct permanent residency — the Investor Pass's one genuinely passive route, with nothing to build or operate.
blocks.bgAlt: Financial district — Paraguay residency by investment

blocks.heading: Residency, without running anything.

paragraphs.text: The financial instruments route qualifies you for the Investor Pass by deploying USD 200,000 — or the guaraní equivalent — into instruments regulated by the securities superintendency of the Central Bank of Paraguay, maintained for a minimum of two years. It is the route for liquid investors who want the residency without a property to manage or a company to run.

There is no business plan, no job-creation obligation, and no active management. You hold the qualifying instruments, file the required annual reporting, and let the framework do its work — making this the only fully passive route to direct permanent residency under Resolution N° 0283/2026.

asideTable.label: Instruments
asideTable.value: Regulated by the BCP securities superintendency

asideTable.label: Evidence
asideTable.value: Certificate from a BCP-authorised entity (≤180 days)

asideNotice.title: Detail still being regulated
asideNotice.body: The exact approved instrument list is being finalised in the regulation of Resolution 0283/2026. We confirm eligibility and structure with Paraguayan counsel before any capital is committed.

blocks.imageAlt: Neoclassical bank building — financial instruments route

cards.title: Genuinely passive
cards.text: No company to operate, no property to manage, no team to hire. You qualify by holding, not by building.

cards.title: Capital stays invested
cards.text: Your USD 200,000 remains your capital in regulated instruments — not a fee or a contribution that disappears.

cards.title: Clean to evidence
cards.text: A certificate from a Central Bank-authorised entity documents the holding — straightforward for the file.

cards.title: No operational footprint
cards.text: Ideal for investors who want the residency and the territorial tax footing without a presence to run.

cards.title: Defined horizon
cards.text: A clear two-year minimum hold — known from the outset and planned into your wider structure.

cards.title: Same destination
cards.text: The financial instruments route leads to the identical residency and citizenship pathway as every other route.

paragraphs.text: The passive route is the cleanest sequence in the programme: deploy the capital into qualifying instruments, certify it, then move through the certificate, residency, and — in time — naturalisation.

notice.title: One honest caveat
notice.body: The capital is genuinely locked for two years and instruments can move in value. We structure the holding to stay comfortably above the threshold for the full term.

items.title: Strategy & eligibility
items.text: We confirm the passive route fits your goals and verify qualifying instruments with Paraguayan counsel.

items.title: Deployment
items.text: The USD 200,000 is deployed into qualifying regulated instruments through an authorised entity.

items.title: Certification
items.text: A certificate evidencing the value and the two-year term is obtained, dated within 180 days of filing.

items.title: Foreign Investor Certificate
items.text: With the file complete, the MIC issues the Foreign Investor Certificate (CIE) within five business days.

items.title: Permanent residency
items.text: We progress the permanent residency process and your Paraguayan cédula.

items.title: Hold & report
items.text: You maintain the holding and annual reporting through the two-year term, building toward naturalisation eligibility.

blocks.pull: Residency you hold, not residency you run.
blocks.bgAlt: Asunción waterfront skyline at dusk, Paraguay
